passage.title: Trip Transfer Map Project
passage.text: The map team wanted children to understand a transfer path more clearly. At first, the group made a transfer chart with too many notes in each section, but children lost track of the next stop when each section showed too much text. Trip leaders said "There are many notes here, and I forget where to change buses." After hearing that, the team split the transfer path into smaller sign sections and placed mini maps at every transfer point. They also placed one extra route card before the final turn so the extra card repeated the final change before confusion could grow. On the next test day, children reached each travel stop more smoothly after the redesign. The team noticed that smaller route sections were easier to follow than one crowded map. Because of that result, the school decided to apply the transfer format to other field trips.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Making a transfer path easier for children to understand
Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
Each section showed too much text
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the group add between each travel step?
choices: Short signs
Color blocks
One simple arrow
Larger arrows
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does confirm mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A person who leads the school
Check that something is correct
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: Repeated route signs reduced confusion during transfers
It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Apply the transfer format to other field trips
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
